title: cadellia pentastylis
intro: Cadellia pentastylis, also known as the stinkwood tree, is a plant native to New Caledonia that belongs to the family Celastraceae. The stinkwood tree has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The extracts from this plant have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and its leaves have been known to have antioxidant properties. Cadellia pentastylis can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
